data.title: Palm Oil Falls Amid Profit Taking — Market Talk
data.description: Palm oil prices fall in early Asian trade, amid mild profit taking ahead of Malaysian Palm Oil Board's September supply and demand data due later in the day, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. Slower purchases from India could also weigh on palm oil prices, it says. However, technical analysis suggests CPO futures may stay firm overall with a supportive tone, it adds. AmInvestment Bank sees support for crude palm oil futures at 4,577 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,609 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives December contract is 2 ringgit lower at 4,589 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Gold on Track for Eight-Week Rally
data.description:
Gold rose to around $3,980 per ounce on Friday, heading for its eighth consecutive weekly gain after notching a record high earlier in the week, supported by economic uncertainties and expectations of US rate cuts.

New York Fed President John Williams said he was open to another rate cut, though concerns over rising inflation may complicate the decision.

This echoed minutes from the latest FOMC meeting, which showed policymakers saw growing risks to the labor market but remained cautious about persistent inflation.

Meanwhile, the US government shutdown, now in its second week, has delayed key data releases and continued to fuel market uncertainty.

On Thursday, bullion retreated from its fresh $4,000 milestone as the US dollar strengthened and investors took profits following news that Israel and Hamas had agreed on the first phase of a ceasefire plan.

data.title: Japan futures poised for weekly gains on adverse weather
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures rose for a third straight session on Friday and were on track to end the week higher, underpinned by a rebound in raw material prices following heavy rainfall in major production areas.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for March delivery , was up 1% at 313.1 yen ($2.05) per kg, as of 0219 GMT.

  • The contract was set to end the week with a gain of 5.17%.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for January delivery fell 0.07% to 15,315 yuan ($2,149.05) per metric ton.

  • The most-active November butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) lost 0.49% to 11,205 yuan per ton.

  • Heavy rainfall in some domestic and international production areas has led to a rebound in raw material prices, supporting fundamentals for natural rubber, said Chinese manufacturing company Jinlianchuang Rubber Group.

  • Top rubber producer Thailand's meteorological agency warned of heavy rains and accumulations from October 13-15.

  • The yen steadied at 153.12 per U.S. dollar, hovering near its weakest level since late February.

  • A weaker Japanese currency makes yen-denominated assets more affordable to overseas buyers.

  • Chinese holidaymakers' average spending fell to a three-year low during the country's Golden Week holiday, offering further evidence of weak consumption in the world's second-largest economy.

  • Indian engineering research and development firm Tata Elxsi (TTEL.NS) reported its fourth straight quarter of declining profits, attributing the downturn to a slowdown in the automotive sector.

  • The company noted that the global automotive industry continues to face uncertainty due to fluctuations in the Chinese market and the impact of tariffs.

  • Automobile sales could influence the intensity of automobile manufacturing, which involves using rubber-made tyres.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for November delivery last traded at 171.5 U.S. cents per kg, down 0.8%.

($1 = 152.9400 yen)

($1 = 7.1264 Chinese yuan)

data.title: New Zealand Dollar Hovers Near 6-Month Low
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar edged up to $0.575 on Friday but remained close to a six-month low, weighed down by the Reserve Bank’s dovish monetary policy.

Earlier in the week, the central bank cut its official cash rate by an outsized 50bps to 2.50%, the lowest level since July 2022, and left the door open to yet further easing amid concerns about the frail state of the economy.

Markets are now pricing in an 80% probability of a 25bps rate cut at the RBNZ’s next meeting in November, with roughly even odds that rates could fall to 2.0% by next year.

Downward pressure on the kiwi was compounded by a stronger US dollar, which is aided by hawkish tone from some Federal Reserve officials, both in the minutes from September’s meeting as well as ongoing commentary.

The New Zealand dollar has declined more than 1% so far this week.

data.title: Fed's Daly: labor market is at a 'worrisome' point
data.description:
Key points:
  • Labor demand is softening, warranting rate cuts: Daly
  • AI could transform economy; current slowdown is speeding adoption
  • Cutting rates is risk management, policy still modestly restrictive: Daly

San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly said on Thursday that a softening labor market and easing inflation were reasons behind last month's decision to cut rates, and prompted the Fed to signal the possibility of further rate cuts ahead.

"The economy is slowing a little bit. Consumers are running out of all the excess savings they might have had and they've been dealing with a higher price level. And then we have restrictive monetary policy," Daly said at an event at the Silicon Valley Directors Exchange.

"We're to a point now where the softening in the labor market looks like it could be more worrisome if we don't risk manage it," Daly said. Even after September's rate cut monetary policy is "modestly restrictive" and putting downward pressure on inflation, she said.

"That interest rate cut we took — and we projected we might take more — those were cuts designed to risk manage as we move both our inflation goal and our employment goal to a more perfect balance," Daly said.

Asked about the impact of artificial intelligence on the economy, Daly said there's good reason to think it could be transformational, though enthusiasts may well be disappointed because typically innovations that transform economies typically take decades to do so.

This time it may be different, she suggested, not just because of the technology itself but also because the economy is slowing. Companies are loathe to hire employees that they might need to fire if demand falls further, and are therefore trying to find ways to use artificial intelligence to plug labor gaps.

"We're now in a forcing function where we might see the gains come faster, not just because of the AI itself and what the technology can do, but also because of where we are in the economy and how firms might be able to use it," Daly said.

data.title: Ten-Year JGB Yield Seen at 1.75% by End-2025, 2.00% by End-2026 — Market Talk
data.description: Capital Economics no longer expects the Bank of Japan to raise rates this month but has kept its forecasts for 10-year JGB yields, says Thomas Mathews, head of markets for Asia-Pacific. The 10-year yield is already close to Capital Economics' end-2025 forecast of 1.75%. "Even if the BOJ didn't raise rates again this year(a small chance of a hike is currently discounted in money markets), we doubt long-dated JGBs would rally much, as it would probably be interpreted as a delay to tightening rather than a cancellation," he says in a note. Because the BOJ is expected to eventually hike more than markets anticipate, Capital Economics maintains its end-2026 forecast of 2.00% for the 10-year yield. It was recently at 1.690%. (colin.ng@wsj.com)

data.title: China Stocks Decline on Weak Global Sentiment
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite fell 0.3% to around 3,920, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 0.8% to 13,613 on Friday, giving back part of the prior session’s gains amid soft global cues.

US stocks tumbled overnight as concerns grew over stretched valuations, particularly among AI-related firms, while investors assessed the impact of the prolonged US government shutdown and the Federal Reserve’s rate outlook.

A gauge of US-listed Chinese firms slid 2% on Thursday, suggesting that the recent rally in mainland shares may be losing steam.

Investors now turn their attention to the Communist Party’s leadership meeting on Oct. 20–23 and a possible Xi-Trump meeting at the APEC summit later this month for new policy signals.

Major decliners included Contemporary Amperex (-5.3%), Luxshare Precision (-3.5%), Wuxi Lead Intelligent (-10.7%), and Zijin Mining (-2.6%).

data.title: Yen Could Strengthen to 140 Against Dollar by End-2026 — Market Talk
data.description: Capital Economics no longer expects the Bank of Japan to raise rates this month, says Thomas Mathews, head of markets for Asia-Pacific. The shift doesn't affect its 10-year JGB yield forecasts but delays the expected yen rebound. Capital Economics now projects USD/JPY to end 2025 at 150, before strengthening to 140 by end-2026 and 135 by end-2027, versus prior forecasts of 140, 135 and 130, respectively. The yen looks extremely weak against the dollar, so it won't take a large rise in JGB yields to trigger a rally, he says. "We suspect a sustained rally is more likely next year, once the Bank resumes hiking in earnest," Mathews says. USD/JPY was recently around 152.96. (colin.ng@wsj.com)

data.title: Anime Tokyo Station: TV Anime 'SPYxFAMILY' Special Exhibition, New Designs Added for Admission Bonus Illustration Cards
data.description:
TOKYO, Oct 10, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - Anime Tokyo Station is an anime exhibition site focused on Japanese anime content, which has developed a devout fan base around the world. Under the motto, "Make ANIME more interesting, Bringing ANIME far into the future," it regularly hosts special exhibitions for popular anime and attracts 214,731 visitors (as of September 15, 2025) from both Japan and overseas as a facility that communicates the appeal of Japanese anime to the world.

Anime Tokyo Station has been holding a special exhibition from August 2025 for the TV anime "SPYxFAMILY", a series popular not just in Japan but the entire world, in anticipation of its third season which is scheduled to begin broadcast in October.

Two new designs will be added to the illustration cards, which are distributed as an admission bonus. The second design of illustration cards to be distributed from September 27 (Sat.) to October 17 (Fri.) is based on key visuals from Season 3 of the TV anime with the adorable Anya in the center. The third design of illustration cards is scheduled to be distributed from October 18 (Sat.) to November 9 (Sun.)

*The third design will be posted on the official website and social media accounts of Anime Tokyo Station once it is finalized.

Additionally, meet-and-greet events with Anya have been confirmed for October 26 (Sun.), which will be limited to 20 persons per session. Here, participants will be able to take commemorative photos as a memory of their time with the adorable Anya. Applications to attend the events are scheduled to become accessible on September 22 (Mon.)

We invite you to visit Anime Tokyo Station and fully enjoy the world of TV anime "SPYxFAMILY" and the appeal of its characters.

Visit the page below for more information on the TV anime "SPYxFAMILY" Special Exhibition.

https://prtimes.jp/main/html/rd/p/000000019.000017625.html

Illustration card distribution periods

2nd design: September 27 (Sat.) to October 17 (Fri.)

3rd design: October 18 (Sat.) to November 9 (Sun.)

*The third design will be posted on the official website and social media accounts of Anime Tokyo Station once it is finalized.

data.title: Dovish Incoming Japan PM Unlikely to Alter BOJ Policy Path — Market Talk
data.description: Sanae Takaichi's election as LDP leader and incoming prime minister has made a splash in markets, but Capital Economics' Thomas Mathews is skeptical she will significantly change the overall monetary policy path, despite dovish statements from her and her advisors. Mathews, head of markets for Asia-Pacific, says BOJ governor Ueda is clearly cautious about rate hikes and is unlikely to surprise markets. "So, given Takaichi's victory and the market response, an October hike--which we'd previously expected--now looks much less likely to us," he says. Capital Economics now expects the next rate increase in January, with rates rising to 1.50% by end-2027, higher than current market expectations. (colin.ng@wsj.com)

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Poised for Steep Weekly Losses
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks dropped 249 points, or 0.9%, to 26,501 in early trade on Friday, marking a fifth straight fall and the lowest in near two weeks.

Sentiment was pressured after Wall Street slipped from record highs overnight, as traders assessed remarks from Fed officials on interest rates and the start of the U.S. earnings season.

Caution also grew ahead of key Chinese data next week, including September trade figures, CPI and PPI readings, and credit statistics.

Most sectors dipped, led by tech after China widened rare earth export controls for tech and military uses.

Financials dipped after HSBC Holdings plans a USD 13.6 billion buyout of minority shareholders in its majority-owned Hang Seng Bank.

Top losers included SMIC (-4.2%), Baidu (-3.4%), Alibaba (-3.0%), and Shenzhou Intl. (-2.5%).

Losses were limited by bullish mood in mainland stocks, which resumed trading Thursday after a week-long break.

Hong Kong is set to end the week sharply lower, following last week’s strong gains.

data.title: Chinese Government Bond Yield Curve's Steepening Could Pause — Market Talk
data.description: The Chinese government bond yield curve's steepening may pause, DBS Group Research's Samuel Tse says in a commentary. He notes the 10-year yield's decline to 1.85% from 1.91% on Thursday, which suggests the recent selloff in China's government bonds could be coming to an end. One of the factors keeping long-end yields steady is that the market appears to have mostly priced in a coming debt issuance, the senior economist says. Another is that inflation remains weak. At the curve's short end, yields should also remain relatively stable. Further downside seems limited, as the PBOC is likely to cut the loan prime rate and seven-day repo rate by only another 10 bps this year, Tse adds. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Bitcoin Correction Deepens – Traders Cautious As Downside Pressure Builds Further
data.description:
Bitcoin price corrected gains and traded below the $124,000 level. BTC is now struggling and might continue to move down below $120,000.

  • Bitcoin started a downside correction below the $123,200 level.
  • The price is trading below $123,000 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average.
  • There is a bearish trend line forming with resistance at $122,750 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair (data feed from Kraken).
  • The pair might continue to move down if it trades below the $120,000 zone.

Bitcoin Price Dips Further

Bitcoin price failed to stay above the $125,000 zone and started a fresh decline. BTC dipped below the $124,000 support to enter a short-term bearish zone.

The bears even pushed the price below $121,200. A low was formed at $119,810 and the price recently recovered some losses. There was a move toward the 50% Fib retracement level of the recent decline from the $123,750 swing high to the $119,810 low.

However, the bears are still active near $121,750. Bitcoin is now trading below $121,500 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average. Besides, there is a bearish trend line forming with resistance at $122,750 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair.

Immediate resistance on the upside is near the $121,750 level. The first key resistance is near the $122,250 level and the 61.8% Fib retracement level of the recent decline from the $123,750 swing high to the $119,810 low.

The next resistance could be $122,750 and the trend line. A close above the $122,750 resistance might send the price further higher. In the stated case, the price could rise and test the $123,500 resistance. Any more gains might send the price toward the $124,000 level. The next barrier for the bulls could be $125,500.

More Losses In BTC?

If Bitcoin fails to rise above the $122,750 resistance zone, it could start a fresh decline. Immediate support is near the $120,750 level. The first major support is near the $120,000 level.

The next support is now near the $118,500 zone. Any more losses might send the price toward the $116,500 support in the near term. The main support sits at $115,500, below which BTC might struggle to recover in the short term.

Technical indicators:

Hourly MACD – The MACD is now gaining pace in the bearish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for BTC/USD is now below the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $120,750, followed by $120,000.

Major Resistance Levels – $122,750 and $123,500.

data.title: Seatrium Shares Extend Losses; Last 6.6% Lower at S$2.28
Monero releases ‘Flourine Fermi’ update to fight spy nodes
data.description:
Privacy blockchain Monero has rolled out a new client update to provide users with greater security against “spy nodes” on the network.

The “Fluorine Fermi” software update was announced via X on Thursday, with the team stating it is “a highly recommended release.”

The term “spy nodes” is part of the vernacular used in the Monero community. It refers to malicious nodes, groups of nodes, or botnets that have the potential to calculate and match IP addresses to transactions conducted on the network. 

The changes focus on providing greater data protection and node matching protocols, such as an improved peer selection algorithm that works to avoid connecting to multiple nodes in the same range of IP addresses and an increase in the limit of subaddresses (unique one-time addresses) that can be created at once, along with some general reliability and stability fixes. 

Monero privacy threats 

The Fluorine Fermi provides another tool for users to protect their privacy. 

Given that spy nodes threaten the privacy of users in contrast to the main aim of the project, the community has worked hard to find workarounds, establish safety practices, promote self-node operation and utilize software to provide a shield against malicious nodes on Monero. 

One such idea, proposed by the Monero Research Lab in late 2024, is to enable node operators to create a ban list of all IP addresses suspected of being spy nodes, thereby avoiding connections to them.

However, this is not seen as a foolproof or fully sustainable tactic, as malicious node operators could just go on and set up new spy IP addresses if they need to. 

Another tool the community promotes is the use of Dandelion++, software designed to prevent malicious actors from linking IP addresses to transactions.

Monero privacy issues were, in part, brought into the spotlight via a leaked Chainalysis video that appeared in September 2024. 

Cointelegraph was supplied with the video by an anonymous source, which claimed that Chainalysis was able to track transactions back to 2021 via its own “malicious” Monero nodes.

Headline: ‘Help! My robot vac is stealing my Bitcoin’: When smart devices attack

data.title: Genting Malaysia's 2H Earnings Could Decline — Market Talk
data.description: Genting Malaysia's 2H earnings could decline amid a high base in 2Q and larger losses from Empire post-consolidation, Affin Hwang IB analyst Andrew Lim says in a note. He tips 2H earnings between MYR200 million and MYR250 million, compared with 1H's MYR489 million. He expects 2025-2026 earnings to be flat on year as core gaming improvements are likely to be offset by higher losses from its Empire Resorts unit post-consolidation. Affin Hwang raises target price to MYR2.15 from MYR1.95 as it updates the valuation multiple, while maintaining a hold rating on the stock. Shares are 0.7% lower at MYR2.86. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Alkane Resources 1Q Production Lower Than Expected — Market Talk
data.description: Alkane's 1Q result is softer than Moelis Australia expected, with all three of the company's gold mines reporting production marginally below the bank's forecasts, say analysts Paul Hissey and Nic McRostie. "There was no individual asset driver, with the variance broadly distributed across the portfolio," resulting in a 3,600-ounce shortfall overall, the analysts say. "We're unconcerned, however, given the nuance of the timing of deal completion and therefore the commencement of aggregated production," they say, referring to Alkane's merger with Mandalay Resources in August. Cash is also lower than forecast, although that appears in part due to higher-than-expected deal costs, they say. Moelis Australia has a buy rating and A$1.50 target on Alkane, which is down 5.2% at A$1.10. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Australia's Guzman y Gomez slips, Morningstar unimpressed with share buyback plan
data.description:
** Mexican-themed fast-food chain Guzman y Gomez slips as much as 4.6% to A$25.180, posting its biggest intraday pct loss since August 28

** Morningstar notes GYG's A$100 mln ($65.49 mln) share buyback with share price trading over A$26

** Investment research firm highlights how GYG issued shares at A$22 each 15 months ago; says selling low and buying high does not create shareholder value

** Morningstar expressed caution about expensive shares, which are trading at 100 times next year's earnings

** Flags no impact on fair value estimate from buyback, maintains PT at A$16 per share

** GYG stock posts record intraday pct jump on Thursday following share buyback plan and results, before closing 2.3% lower at A$26.39

** Stock last down 1.8%, taking YTD losses to 36.3%

($1 = 1.5270 Australian dollars)

data.title: Top Glove Faces Heightened Competition From Expanding China Capacity — Market Talk
data.description: Top Glove may continue to face stiff competition as Chinese glove makers expand into Southeast Asia and adopt aggressive pricing strategies to regain U.S. market share, Maybank IB analyst Wong Wei Sum says in a note. Competition is expected to intensify with new Chinese capacity coming online from November, but should begin to stabilize by FY 2027 as the additional supply is absorbed, she says. Management targets 20%-30% sales volume growth in FY 2026 and expects margins to improve further as plant utilization reaches 80%-90%. Maybank maintains a sell rating on Top Glove and keeps its target price at MYR0.61. Shares are 3.7% higher at MYR0.70.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: India stock benchmarks set to open higher on strong TCS earnings, renewed FPI inflows
data.description:
India's equity benchmarks are set to open marginally higher on Friday, with focus on information technology stocks after Tata Consultancy Services, the country's No. 1 software services firm, posted better-than-expected second-quarter revenue.

Gift Nifty futures (GIFc1) were trading at 25,245 points as of 7:49 a.m. IST, indicating that the benchmark Nifty 50 will open above Thursday's close of 25,181.8.

Nifty 50 has risen in five of the past six sessions, gaining 2.3%, led by about 6% rally in IT stocks .

The sector will remain in focus after TCS beat September-quarter revenue estimates, driven by strength in its banking, financial services and insurance (BFSI) segment.

The company said it expects stronger growth in the second half of fiscal 2026.

However, profits missed estimates, weighed down by a surge in severance costs after the company announced plans to cut 2% of its workforce this fiscal year.

While TCS's results were largely in line, the real catalyst for sentiment was its announcement of a new entity to build world-class artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure, analysts said.

Meanwhile, market sentiment was also boosted by the return of foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) to net buying over the past three sessions.

On Thursday, foreign institutional investors (FIIs) bought shares worth 13.08 billion rupees ($147.3 million) on a net basis, per the National Stock Exchange's provisional data.

Domestic institutional investors have remained net buyers for 32 straight sessions.

While the secondary market showed resilience, the primary market also saw a surge in activity, highlighted by the $1.3 billion maiden share sale by LG Electronics' Indian unit - the most subscribed billion-dollar IPO in nearly two decades.

STOCKS TO WATCH

** Tata Elxsi's (TTEL.NS) profit fell for the fourth straight quarter as the company's transportation business declined amid uncertain markets and geopolitical tensions.

** Mahindra and Mahindra says its total production volume rose 24.4% year-on-year to 99,758 units in September.

** Afcons Infrastructure gets contract worth 5.76 billion rupees.

** NTPC Green Energy signs deal with Gujarat government for solar and wind projects.

($1 = 88.7930 Indian rupees)

data.title: ANZ Forecasts Oil Prices To Trade Around $60-65/BBL Through 1H26
data.description:
ANZ:

  • SUPPLY-DRIVEN SURPLUSES ARE RARELY UNEXPECTED, SO THE MARKET USUALLY PRICES-IN SURPLUS WELL IN ADVANCE, CONSEQUENTLY, EXPECT OIL PRICES TO TRADE AROUND $60–65/BBL, THROUGH FIRST HALF OF 2026

  • FURTHER RECOVERY OF OIL PRICES TO $70/BBL BY END OF 2026 IS POSSIBLE; EITHER DUE TO RISING RECOVERY IN DEMAND OR OPEC STEPPING IN TO SUPPORT MARKET WITH PRODUCTION CUTS

data.title: Indonesia issues tsunami warning after Philippines quake
data.description:
Indonesia has issued a tsunami warning for northern Sulawesi and Papua regions after a strong earthquake hit the southern Philippines on Friday, Indonesia's geophysics agency said.

The agency's modelling suggested there was a risk of tsunami waves as high as 50 cm hitting Indonesia's shorelines, it said in a statement.

data.title: Yen Heads for Worst Week in a Year
data.description:
The Japanese yen traded at an eight-month low around 153 per dollar on Friday and was on track to decline nearly 4% for the week, marking its steepest decline since September last year.

The currency weakened after fiscal dove Sanae Takaichi won Japan’s leadership race to become the next prime minister, fueling expectations of higher fiscal spending and loose monetary policy.

Takaichi said Thursday that the Bank of Japan would set policy independently while aligning with the government’s goals, adding she aimed to avoid excessive yen depreciation, although her comments that did little to support the currency.

Markets now see less than a 50% chance of a BOJ rate hike in December, with expectations shifting toward March next year.

Meanwhile, data showed producer prices in Japan rose more than expected in September, further complicating the rate outlook.

data.title: South Korean shares resume record rally on chipmaker boost
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI rises, foreigners net buyers
  • Korean won weakens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield rises

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares resumed their record-setting rally on Friday following a local holiday break, underpinned by chipmakers, as optimism around artificial intelligence continued to drive gains.

** The won weakened to a five-month low, while the benchmark bond yield rose.

** The benchmark KOSPI was up 63.95 points, or 1.80%, at 3,613.16, as of 0135 GMT. Earlier in the day, the index climbed as much as nearly 2% to an all-time high.

** Chipmaker Samsung Electronics rose 5.96% to its highest level since January 2021 and peer SK Hynix gained 9.23% to its highest point on record.

** The South Korean chipmakers, which signed a supply deal with OpenAI just before the local holiday period from October 3 to October 9, extended gains for a third consecutive session.

** However, risk appetite remained weak across broader financial markets, with the country's central bank stating that it would closely monitor conditions as global risk factors had slightly increased over the holiday period.

** Shares of steelmaker POSCO Holdings shed 3.85% and Hyundai Steel dropped 1.36%, after the European Union announced higher tariffs on steel imports.

** Among other index heavyweights, battery maker LG Energy Solution slid 10.09%, while Hyundai Motor and sister automaker Kia Corp were down 1.14% and 2.69%, respectively.

** Of the total 929 traded issues, 250 shares advanced, while 649 declined.

** Foreigners were net buyers of shares worth 655.8 billion won ($461.53 million).

** The won was quoted at 1,419.6 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.89% lower than its previous close at 1,407.0. During the session, it hit its weakest level since mid-May at 1,424.0.

** In money and debt markets, December futures on three-year treasury bonds (KTBc1) lost 0.09 point to 106.70.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) rose by 3.0 basis points to 2.608%, while the benchmark 10-year yield rose by 2.4 basis points to 2.981%.

($1 = 1,420.9200 won)

data.title: Shanghai Zhida Technology shines in Hong Kong debut
data.description:
** Shares of Shanghai Zhida Technology Development (2650.HK) trade as high as HK$194 in Hong Kong debut

** Stock up 189.9% from offer price of HK$66.92 apiece

** Stock trims gains to HK$191 each in early morning trade, still up 185.4% with 1.2 million shares worth HK$220.9 million ($28.39 million) changing hands

** The Chinese smart home electric vehicle (EV) chargers and accessories producer sold 5.98 million shares in global offering, raising HK$400 million ($51.41 million) for overseas expansion, upgrading production facilities and R&D, among others

** Says subscription level of its Hong Kong public offering portion is 5,440.80 times, while international offering portion is 2.37 times

** The Hang Seng Tech Index drops 2.2% and benchmark Hang Seng Index falls 1.3%

($1 = 7.7806 Hong Kong dollars)
